title: Model correctness + consistency checks (validator/graph/scaffold)
description: Fix correctness and consistency gaps that make models drift silently: ensure validation detects contexts present on disk but missing from the index; make graph edge generation deterministic and avoid duplicate edges that skew stats/health; clarify and harden new-domain --force behavior (replace vs merge); improve summary output to include essential fields for quick reasoning; and tighten search/tag semantics so glossary aliases and tag filters behave predictably.
notes: Acceptance criteria: Validation detects contexts present on disk but missing from the domain index and fails (or warns) deterministically; graph generation and health checks are not skewed by duplicate edges; new-domain --force behavior is explicit and does not silently merge unrelated content; summary output includes essential high-signal fields for quick reasoning without requiring a follow-up show.

title: Add init and prime CLI commands for agent onboarding
description: Add two new CLI commands: dkk init (creates/updates AGENTS.md) and dkk prime (outputs full agent context)

title: Implement dkk init command
description: Create src/features/agent/commands/init.ts. Command creates or updates AGENTS.md at repo root with a DKK section delimited by HTML comment markers. Idempotent merge: replace between markers if found, append if not, create if missing. Concise content pointing to dkk prime.
acceptance_criteria: Creates AGENTS.md when none exists. Appends DKK section preserving existing content. Idempotent on re-run. Prints created/updated confirmation.

title: Implement dkk prime command
description: Create src/features/agent/commands/prime.ts. Outputs comprehensive DKK usage context to stdout for agent consumption. Hardcoded template: project overview, core principles, domain structure, retrieval workflow, change workflow, ID conventions, CLI reference, file conventions. Uses dkk as CLI name.
acceptance_criteria: Outputs full agent context to stdout with exit 0. Contains all key sections. Uses dkk as CLI name.

title: Wire init and prime into CLI entry point
description: Import registerInit and registerPrime in src/cli.ts and register as top-level commands.
acceptance_criteria: dkk --help lists init and prime. Both commands are callable.

title: Add integration tests for init and prime
description: Add test sections in test/cli-integration.ts. Init tests: create new, append existing, idempotent re-run. Prime tests: stdout output, key sections present. Update smoke test to include init and prime in --help output check.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes with all new and existing tests green.

title: Dogfood: run dkk init on project AGENTS.md
description: Run dkk init on the project itself. Verify AGENTS.md has the DKK section with markers alongside existing bd/session content.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md contains both existing content and DKK section with comment markers.

title: CLI Display & Help Ergonomics: Types, Help Width, and Kit Rename
description: 1. Update top-level description from Pack to Kit. Configure Commander width to prevent wrapping. Shorten long descriptions. 2. Expose Types (event, command, policy, aggregate, read_model, etc.) & ID Formats explicitly at root help. 3. Normalize 'read-model' token everywhere to 'read_model' but allow 'read-model' as alias.

title: DKK Usability Overhaul
description: Improve DKK usage simplicity without decreasing features. Covers critical bug fixes (path resolution, doc/code format mismatch), workflow streamlining (eliminate double validation, auto-build search index), scaffolding commands (dkk new, dkk add for AI agent consumption), and enriching dkk prime with live domain data. Goal: reduce onboarding from 8+ manual steps to 1 command, and the daily quality gate from 2 commands to 1.

comments.author: Kaan Akın
comments.text: Preflight complete. Epic decomposed into 7 children (783.1-783.7). All children added as blocking deps so epic won't appear in bd ready until all children are closed. Inter-child dep: 783.5 blocks 783.6. Suggested order: 783.1+783.2 (P1 bugs), then 783.3+783.4 (P2 tasks), then 783.5->783.6 (features), 783.7 (independent P3).

title: Fix repoRoot() path resolution for installed packages
description:
src/shared/paths.ts resolves repoRoot() via import.meta.dirname, which points to the DKK package install directory — not the user's project. Every command silently looks for .dkk/domain/ inside node_modules/domain-knowledge-kit/. The --root flag is effectively mandatory but documented as optional.

Fix: Split path resolution into two strategies:
- Domain paths (domainRoot, docsRoot, searchIndexPath) resolve from process.cwd() or --root override
- Package asset paths (schemaDir, templateDir) resolve from import.meta.dirname relative to the package install

Files to change:
- src/shared/paths.ts — add packageRoot() helper, change repoRoot() default to process.cwd()
- src/features/pipeline/validator.ts — use packageRoot() for schema resolution
- src/features/pipeline/renderer.ts — use packageRoot() for template resolution
- src/features/pipeline/indexer.ts — u  --type bug   --priority 1   --parent domain-knowledge-kit-783 r  --priorityUpd  --parent domari  -d src/shared/paths.ts

title: Fix documentation to match per-item directory format
description:
All user-facing docs show a single-file context format (ordering.yml with inline events/commands), but src/shared/loader.ts only supports the per-item directory format (contexts/ordering/context.yml + events/OrderPlaced.yml). Users following the docs get zero contexts loaded silently.

Also: aggregate docs show flat handles/emits arrays but the actual schema requires nested handles.commands/emits.events format.

Files to change:
- README.md — update quick-start YAML examples to directory structure
- docs/getting-started.md — rewrite steps 1-3 with correct per-item file layout
- docs/domain-modeling.md — update examples for each item type
- docs/cli-reference.md — fix any examples using old format
- src/features/agent/commands/prime.ts — update hardcoded YAML structure reference
- Fix aggregate examples to show correct nested handles.commands/emits.events for  --type bug   --priority 1   --parent domain-knowledge-kit-78in  --priority ac  --parent doma v  -d All

title: Streamline quality gate — eliminate double validation
description:
Docs and AGENTS.md instruct users to run 'dkk validate' then 'dkk render'. But render.ts already calls validateDomainModel() internally (unless --skip-validation). Every domain change triggers validation twice.

Fix: Update all documentation to recommend just 'dkk render' as the single quality gate command. Keep 'dkk validate' available as a standalone dry-run check (useful for CI or quick validation without render), but position it as optional/lightweight.

Files to change:
- AGENTS.md — quality gates section: recommend just dkk render
- docs/getting-started.md — update quality gate instructions
- docs/domain-modeling.md — update quality gate references
- src/features/agent/commands/prime.ts — update hardcoded quality gate instructions
- src/features/agent/commands/init.ts — update AGENTS.md template quality gate section
- .github/copilot-instructions.m  --type task   --priority 2   --parent domain-knowledge-kit-783 is  --priority 2nd  --parent domas   -d Docs

title: Auto-build search index on dkk search
description:
dkk search fails with "Search index not found. Run render first." when no index exists. Users must know to run render before searching - a hidden prerequisite.

Fix: When the search index is not found, automatically build it by calling the indexer before executing the search.

Files to change:
- src/features/query/commands/search.ts - catch missing index, call buildSearchIndex() as fallback
- src/features/pipeline/indexer.ts - ensure buildSearchIndex can be imported and called standalone
- Add a --no-auto-index flag for fail-fast behavior
- Add test case for the auto-build path

Implementation:
1. Import buildSearchIndex from indexer.ts
2. In the search command, wrap the searcher call to catch the "not found" error
3. When caught, print "Search index not found - building... done." and retry
4. Add --no-auto-index flag to preserve old behavior for scripts that want explicit control
acceptance_criteria: Running dkk search on a project with valid domain YAML but no prior render succeeds after auto-building the index. A brief message informs the user the index was built.
notes: Implementation was already in search.ts (auto-build + --no-auto-index flag). Updated E2E tests in test/cli-integration.ts: (1) replaced old 'search fails when no index' test with 'search auto-builds index when missing' — asserts exit 0, finds results, prints building message to stderr; (2) added '--no-auto-index fails on missing index' test — asserts exit 1 and error message; (3) fixed run() helper to use spawnSync so stderr is captured on success. All 121 E2E tests pass, typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: Add dkk new scaffolding command
description:
Setting up a domain model requires 8+ manual steps: create directories, write index.yml, actors.yml, context.yml, item files, etc. No guidance, no templates, easy to get the structure wrong.

Fix: Add scaffolding subcommands under "dkk new":

- dkk new domain - creates full .dkk/domain/ structure: index.yml, actors.yml, contexts/ directory, and one example context with a sample event, command, and aggregate
- dkk new context <name> - creates contexts/<name>/context.yml + type subdirectories (events/, commands/, aggregates/, policies/, read-models/, glossary/) and registers the context in index.yml
- dkk new adr <title> - creates next .dkk/adr/adr-NNNN.md with frontmatter template

All subcommands use flags (not interactive prompts) for AI agent compatibility:
- dkk new context ordering --description "Order lifecycle"
- dkk new adr "Adopt Event Sourcing" --status accepted

Files to create/change:
- src/features/scaffold/commands/new.ts - new command module
- src/features/scaffold/templates/ - YAML template strings (or inline them)
- src/cli.ts - register new commands
- Add integration tests
acceptance_criteria: dkk new domain && dkk render succeeds from an empty project. AI agents can run dkk new context <name> --description '...' without interactive prompts.

title: Add dkk add item scaffolding command
description:
Adding individual domain items (events, commands, aggregates, policies, read-models, glossary entries) requires knowing the exact directory structure and YAML schema. Agents and users must create files manually.

Depends on: Ticket 5 (dkk new scaffolding) for shared scaffold infrastructure.

Fix: Add "dkk add" subcommands for each item type:
- dkk add event <context> <name> [--description "..."] [--raised-by <aggregate>] [--triggers <policy1,policy2>]
- dkk add command <context> <name> [--description "..."] [--handled-by <aggregate>]
- dkk add aggregate <context> <name> [--description "..."] [--handles <cmd1,cmd2>] [--emits <evt1,evt2>]
- dkk add policy <context> <name> [--description "..."] [--triggered-by <event>] [--emits-command <cmd>]
- dkk add read-model <context> <name> [--description "..."] [--updated-by <evt1,evt2>]
- dkk add glossary <context> <term> [--definition "..."]
- dkk add actor <name> [--description "..."] (appends to actors.yml)

Design:
- Each creates the correctly-structured YAML file in the right directory
- Validates the target context exists; errors clearly if not
- All flags are optional - creates minimal valid YAML with just the name if no flags provided
- Flag-based interface optimized for AI agent consumption

Files to create/change:
- src/features/scaffold/commands/add.ts - new command module
- src/cli.ts - register add commands
- Add unit and integration tests
acceptance_criteria: dkk add event ordering OrderPlaced --description 'Raised when order is placed' --raised-by Order creates a valid YAML file that passes dkk validate.

title: Enrich dkk prime with live domain summary
description:
dkk prime outputs a hardcoded generic instruction document. Agents get a user manual but zero project-specific domain knowledge. They must then run dkk list, dkk show, etc. separately to learn the actual domain model.

Fix: After the static instructions, dynamically append a "Current Domain Summary" section:
- Load the domain model via loader.ts
- Count and list contexts with their descriptions
- Count items per type (events, commands, aggregates, policies, read-models, glossary)
- List actors
- List ADRs with titles and statuses
- Show key relationships (aggregates and their handled commands, emitted events)

Use item-visitor.ts to iterate items efficiently.

Graceful degradation: if no domain model exists or loading fails, output just the static instructions with a note: "No domain model found. Run dkk new domain to get started."

Add --static-only flag for cases where only the generic instructions are wanted.

Files to change:
- src/features/agent/commands/prime.ts - add dynamic domain summary generation
- src/shared/loader.ts - ensure loadAllContexts can be called from prime without side effects
- src/shared/item-visitor.ts - may need summary-oriented visitor helpers
- Add tests for dynamic summary generation
acceptance_criteria: Running dkk prime on a project with a domain model outputs both the instruction manual AND a summary showing contexts, item counts, actors, and ADRs. On an empty project, it outputs instructions plus a helpful no-domain-found note.
notes:
Preflight: prime.ts outputs a hardcoded string via primeContent(). No --root flag. loadDomainModel() in loader.ts accepts {root?} and returns DomainModel with index, actors, contexts (Map), adrs (Map). forEachItem iterates items. Plan: add --root and --static-only opts, generate dynamic summary after static output. 4 E2E tests.
Verification: typecheck ✓, lint ✓, build ✓, vitest (pre-existing empty suite failures only) ✓, E2E 232/232 ✓. Manual: dkk prime shows summary with ADR from repo, --static-only skips summary. Changes: prime.ts now loads domain model after static output and appends dynamic summary. Added --root and --static-only flags. 5 new E2E tests in cli-integration.ts.

title: Support flow swimlane diagrams in graph and docs
description:
Implement the ability to generate Mermaid.js swimlane/sequence diagrams for domain flows. Add a `--type swimlane` option to the `dkk graph` command and embed these flow diagrams into the generated documentation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- `dkk graph --type swimlane` generates a valid Mermaid sequence/swimlane diagram for flows.
- Running `dkk render` automatically embeds flow sequence diagrams into the generated documentation for flow items.
- The sequence diagram correctly models actors/contexts and their interactions defined in flow YAML.

title: Agent Optimizations: DKK_AGENT_MODE and Locate Capabilities
description: 1. Introduce DKK_AGENT_MODE=1 to globally default outputs to minified JSON, shallow graph traversals, and skip colors. 2. Create 'dkk locate <id>' or enrich 'show/summary --json' output with absoluteFilePath so agents can easily navigate to definitions.

title: Automatic bidirectional wiring for relationships (add + ADR)
description: Reduce manual graph maintenance by automatically wiring both sides of relationships when scaffolding: (1) when creating items with relationship flags, update the referenced items so handles/emits/trigger subscriptions stay consistent; (2) when creating an ADR with domain refs, update referenced domain items' adr_refs to keep bidirectional linkage; (3) enhance cloning so --from can optionally carry relationship wiring; (4) add first-class scaffolding for actors to avoid hand-editing global files and bypassing guardrails.
notes: Acceptance criteria: Creating items with relationship flags results in consistent bidirectional wiring (e.g., raised_by/handled_by/when/then/subscribes_to/used_by reflected on the counterpart structures where applicable); creating an ADR with domain refs updates referenced items' adr_refs automatically; automation is idempotent (no duplicate list entries) and has a safe opt-out if needed.

title: Agent Recovery: Actionable Validation Errors
description: Enhance validation and parsing errors with explicit suggestions (e.g. 'Invalid context paymnts. Did you mean payments?'). Helpful for LLM self-correction. Focus on type, context, and ID lookups.

title: Enforce DKK CLI for updates in AI instructions
description: Update AI instructions to mandate using the dkk CLI commands for all operations on the domain model, explicitly forbidding manual edits to the underlying YAML files.

title: CLI Input Simplification: Flatten Namespace & Streamline Scaffold
description: 1. Remove the 'adr' sub-command group (manage via generic 'show' and 'related' with auto-routing based on 'adr-' prefix). 2. Remove granular relation flags from 'dkk add' (e.g. '--raised-by', '--emits', '--from'). Leave just the basic scaffolding and rely on agent file-editing.

title: Refactor commands: safe rm/rename + move across contexts/types
description: Make refactor commands YAML-aware and reference-safe: support removing/renaming contexts, actors, ADRs, and flows; fix glossary remove/rename to operate on structured YAML entries; ensure --force removal cannot leave dangling references (either cleans them or refuses); add a first-class move capability to relocate items across contexts safely.
notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- Removing/renaming a context updates all affected item IDs and index registrations, and does not leave broken references.
- Removing/renaming an actor updates all references from commands/read-models and does not leave broken references.
- Removing/renaming an ADR updates all domain item adr_refs and ADR cross-links (including superseded relationships) safely.
- Removing/renaming a flow updates index flow step refs and does not leave broken references.
- Glossary remove/rename operates on parsed YAML and removes/updates the full glossary entry block without corrupting context.yml.
- Force removal cannot leave validation failures without an explicit user override; behavior is documented and deterministic.
- A move capability exists to relocate an item across contexts while updating IDs and all references (including flows and ADR domain_refs).

title: Clarify that Event/Command domain modeling does not imply Event-Driven Architecture
description:
AI tools often conflate the presence of 'Events' and 'Commands' in domain models (originating from Event Storming) with architectural decisions like Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) or CQRS. We need to explicitly instruct agents via our CLI (`dkk prime`, `dkk init`) and documentation that mapping these concepts is strictly for understanding the business domain, and they should not infer or suggest EDA/CQRS implementations based solely on this terminology.

Acceptance Criteria:
- `dkk prime` output explicitly warns agents that Events/Commands are for domain modeling, not an EDA/CQRS architectural decision.
- `dkk init` template includes a concise version of this rule for `AGENTS.md`.
- Supporting DKK documentation mentions this distinction.
- CLI commands build and run successfully.

title: CLI JSON/minify parity + doc accuracy
description: Align CLI output flags and documentation: ensure all query/audit commands that support --json also support --minify consistently; add machine-readable JSON output to stats; ensure search/list/show/related honor documented flags; and fix any CLI reference mismatches so agents/CI can rely on stable, token-efficient outputs.
notes: Acceptance criteria: Commands that emit JSON also support --minify consistently; stats supports --json (and --minify where applicable) with a stable schema; CLI reference matches actual flags/behavior; JSON outputs remain backward compatible or are versioned/documented when changed.

title: Instruct AI to prioritize ADRs in decision making
description: Update agent instructions (dkk init and dkk prime) to explicitly direct AI models to consult Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) early in the decision-making and architectural refactor workflow. The current AI agents are not addressing them.
